hooks.command: BRANCH=$(git branch --show-current 2>/dev/null); DEF=$(git symbolic-ref --short refs/remotes/origin/HEAD 2>/dev/null | sed 's|^origin/||'); DEF=${DEF:-main}; if [ -n "$BRANCH" ] && [ "$BRANCH" = "$DEF" ]; then echo "⚠️ On $DEF — create a feature branch before changes (git checkout -b feat/<desc>)."; fi
hooks.description: Remind to use a feature branch when on the default branch

hooks.command: INPUT=$(cat); CMD=$(printf '%s' "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.command // ""' 2>/dev/null || printf '%s' "$INPUT"); D=$(printf '%s' "$CMD" | sed -n 's/^ *cd  *\([^;&|]*\).*/\1/p' | sed 's/"//g; s/ *$//'); if [ -z "$D" ]; then D=$(printf '%s' "$CMD" | sed -n 's/.*git -C  *\([^ ;&|]*\).*/\1/p' | sed 's/"//g'); fi; D=${D:-.}; DEF=$(git -C "$D" symbolic-ref --short refs/remotes/origin/HEAD 2>/dev/null | sed 's|^origin/||'); DEF=${DEF:-main}; case "$CMD" in *'git commit'*) echo '📝 Commit format: type(scope): description — end with a Co-Authored-By trailer.';; esac; ISPUSH=0; case "$CMD" in *'git push'*|*'git -C '*' push'*) ISPUSH=1;; esac; if [ "$ISPUSH" = 1 ]; then BRANCH=$(git -C "$D" branch --show-current 2>/dev/null); if [ "$BRANCH" = "$DEF" ]; then echo "❌ BLOCKED: never push while checked out on $DEF (target repo: $D) — work on a feature branch; branch-switch and push in SEPARATE commands (checks read pre-execution state)." >&2; exit 2; fi; if printf '%s' "$CMD" | grep -qE ":(refs/heads/)?$DEF([^A-Za-z0-9_/-]|$)"; then echo "❌ BLOCKED: push refspec targets $DEF — direct pushes to the default branch are never allowed (even with delegated merge authority); open a PR." >&2; exit 2; fi; case "$CMD" in *'--tags'*|*'--follow-tags'*) echo '⚠️ Tag push may trigger a release/deploy pipeline — per /release, the human runs it.';; esac; if git -C "$D" status --porcelain --untracked-files=no 2>/dev/null | grep -q .; then echo '⚠️ Tracked files have uncommitted modifications — confirm they belong to a later commit before pushing.'; fi; fi; case "$CMD" in *'gh pr merge'*) TOP=$(git -C "$D" rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null); if [ -n "$TOP" ] && grep -qE '\| *\*\*Merge policy\*\* *\| *agent-may-merge' "$TOP/docs/WORKFLOW.md" 2>/dev/null; then echo '⚠️ Delegated merge authority (§10 Merge policy) — merge only on a reviewer APPROVE (or §13 bookkeeping scope) and log it in the ledger.'; else echo '❌ BLOCKED: the human (HITL) merges PRs. Delegating needs Merge policy = agent-may-merge in the TARGET repo docs/WORKFLOW.md §10 — run as cd <repo> && gh pr merge … (fail closed).' >&2; exit 2; fi;; esac; case "$CMD" in *'gh pr create'*) echo '📋 Run the project gates before the PR (test + typecheck/lint; build if release-bound).';; esac; exit 0
hooks.description: git/gh guardrails, evaluated in the command's TARGET repo (leading `cd <dir>` or `git -C <dir>`, else session cwd): block push on/into the default branch (incl. refspecs); policy-aware gh pr merge block; tag-push, commit-format, pre-PR reminders; dirty-tracked-tree warning

hooks.command: INPUT=$(cat); CMD=$(printf '%s' "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.command // ""' 2>/dev/null || printf '%s' "$INPUT"); D=$(printf '%s' "$CMD" | sed -n 's/^ *cd  *\([^;&|]*\).*/\1/p' | sed 's/"//g; s/ *$//'); D=${D:-.}; TOP=$(git -C "$D" rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null); WF="$TOP/docs/WORKFLOW.md"; if printf '%s' "$CMD" | grep -qiE 'ads-api\.|googleads|adaccounts|/act_[0-9]+/|/ads/'; then if ! printf '%s' "$CMD" | grep -q 'PAID_CONFIRMED_BY_HUMAN'; then echo '❌ BLOCKED: paid promotion crosses the money boundary (§11) — never delegable; it must be human-fired within the budget ceiling. No autonomous ad spend.' >&2; exit 2; fi; fi; if printf '%s' "$CMD" | grep -qiE 'api\.(x|twitter|linkedin)\.com|dev\.to/api|buttondown|mailchimp|bsky\.social|hashnode|/api/v1/statuses'; then if grep -qiE '\*\*Publish policy\*\* *\| *_?may-publish' "$WF" 2>/dev/null; then echo '📣 Publishing under a delegated Publish policy (§14) — post only approved, due, organic queue items within scope; append each to publish-log.md.'; else echo '📣 Publishing is §14-gated: Publish policy is not delegated — post only an approved queue item via a human-fired /publish run, and log it. Paid stays human-fired.'; fi; fi; exit 0
hooks.description: publishing guardrail (§14): hard-block paid/ad-spend endpoints unless human-confirmed (never delegable); remind that organic publish-host calls must be policy-authorized, approved queue items logged to publish-log.md

hooks.command: INPUT=$(cat); F=$(printf '%s' "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // ""' 2>/dev/null); L=$(printf '%s' "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.limit // ""' 2>/dev/null); if [ -n "$F" ] && [ -z "$L" ] && [ -f "$F" ]; then N=$(wc -l < "$F" 2>/dev/null | tr -d ' '); if [ -n "$N" ] && [ "$N" -gt 800 ]; then echo "📖 Large whole-file read ($N lines) — prefer a ranged read (offset/limit) or delegate to a subagent that returns a bounded distillate (§2 / §6.2). The main agent ingests conclusions, not corpora."; fi; fi; exit 0
hooks.description: context-discipline backstop (§2/§6.2): advisory reminder when a whole-file Read (no limit) targets a file over ~800 lines — prefer a ranged read or a subagent that returns a distillate; never blocks (exit 0)

hooks.command: INPUT=$(cat); FILEPATH=$(printf '%s' "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // ""' 2>/dev/null || printf '%s' "$INPUT"); case "$FILEPATH" in *CLAUDE.md*|*AGENTS.md*|*docs/WORKFLOW.md*|*docs/architecture*|*docs/data-model*|*schema.prisma*|*schema.sql*) echo '📚 High-impact file changed — update related docs in the same PR (stale-doc rule).';; esac; exit 0
hooks.description: Docs reminder when high-impact files change

hooks.command: git status --porcelain --untracked-files=no 2>/dev/null | grep -q . && echo '⚠️ Session ending with uncommitted tracked changes.' || true
hooks.description: Warn about uncommitted tracked work at session end
